title: Unranked, systemd, crawls
excerpt: There’s been some changes to Marginalia Search: Migrated to systemd from docker New unranked query endpoint Wide domains got their own index for faster crawls In brief, this has removed a lot of operational headaches, reduced expensive queries leaving more computational power for the rest, and cut crawl times in half. Let’s tackle them in some order. We have docker at home The system has been migrated off docker compose, and onto bare systemd in production. This has been successful, and solved a number of issues.
content_html:
There’s been some changes to Marginalia Search:

  • Migrated to systemd from docker
  • New unranked query endpoint
  • Wide domains got their own index for faster crawls

In brief, this has removed a lot of operational headaches, reduced expensive queries leaving more computational power for the rest, and cut crawl times in half.

Let’s tackle them in some order.

We have docker at home

The system has been migrated off docker compose, and onto bare systemd in production. This has been successful, and solved a number of issues.

There are a few constraints that demand a non-standard deployment for Marginalia.

NUMA (non-uniform memory architecture).

The production server has two CPUs, each with their own RAM bank, and while they can reach over into each other’s memory, this comes at a cost.
In many scenarios this isn’t a big deal, but for indexes and databases, generally bottlenecked by RAM bandwidth, this is far from ideal.

Process lifecycles.

A search engine is anything but stateless, some processes are fairly heavy and run for weeks, some services are very slow to start, some services need to restart relatively often.

This all but demands that the search engine is cut up into different pieces.
Not necessarily microservices, but at least services.

IP addresses.

The crawler (and crawler-adjacent processes) run off about a dozen public IP addresses, from the same host. This is done via ipvlan using network namespaces.

Linux has many advanced capabilities for giving processes their own virtual network stack, which can be wired together with virtual switches and virtual patch cables. There are to my knowledge no tools for managing this I would consider good, it’s all either too low level, or abstracting away key functionality, or suffering from multiple sources of truths leading to jank when they inevitably differ.

Docker can deal with all of these constraints, but at the expense of considerable jank.

Docker’s networking model in particular, while it supports ipvlan, doesn’t map onto it particularly well, if you have a finite subnet you pretty much have to have spare free IPs to be able to reliably restart services. It further doesn’t let you set firewall rules for the ipvlan interface, which means that anything you put there better not bind on the public IP, or its ass is going to be hanging out in full view. This is in no way a limitation of ipvlans, but purely a docker problem.

This is made worse by the fact that there is inadequate tooling for letting the processes in the container know which network interface is public, so you have to kinda guess based on dowsing rods, RFC 1918, and looking at tea leaves. This is no way to live.

There is no magic in docker, it’s just cgroups and namespaces all the way down, and you can fully replicate what it’s doing yourself, if you really want to torment yourself using syscalls or shellscripts, or if you want a middle ground using systemd.

If docker’s problem is that there is a mismatch between the docker model of the system and the system itself, systemd represents a more raw approach, where a lot more of the configuration is in your hands, while still offering many capabilities you’d want for a more serious deployment, such as health checks, automatic restarts that can be configured.

Setting up a million .service:s is pretty tedious, but drop-ins help. There isn’t a ton to say here, other than after some fiddling, it works very well, and feels a lot more stable than docker ever did. Cutting JIB out of builds means builds now take 2-3 seconds for the most part.

Deployments are faster, there’s less jank in service discovery because “containers” retain the same internal and external IP, the whole operation feels considerably less floaty. Good upgrade.

There are many opinions about systemd, and I think for most desktop-type systems, it’s incredibly overengineered and kind of a pain to work with. Though for something like this, its design is well motivated, and it really shines.

Unranked Queries

The index now allows unranked query execution!

The query execution pipeline used in the search engine is fairly computationally expensive, and does things that aren’t strictly necessary in many queries.

If you’re for example just looking for backlinks, a pure intersection of the terms ‘site:foo.com links:bar.com’ is enough, there’s nothing to rank anything there, but things working the way they did, threads were allocated to ranking none the less, term positions were attempted to be retrieved, all a bunch of done that needed not to be done.

So I added a path for unranked queries that just do dumb term intersections and a bare minimum of anything else.

The bonus of this is that with some fiddling and the construction of a cursor that can map to multiple index partitions, unlike the ranked path, unranked queries can be exhaustively retrieved with minimal additional work.

Keeping track of the position across multiple partitions to allow exhaustive retrieval took some thinking, but I designed a cursor that looks like this, that can be passed to the clients:

28mbshfptkj.6ijoop7xty.43nivb3bn1.817ucldxy2x.90.12ria2fmp7a.32cydk0dei2t.73wrhi4igjr.53lb1o4iof7

Each period-delimited part starts with a single character mapping to an index partition, and then a string of alphanumerical values that’s a base 36-encoded document id, the document id to resume from on that partition. It’s short enough to pass on a query string or along an API query, which is really all that matters.

Implementing this allows up to half of the query load to go to the new endpoint, though many of these queries are driven by bots and scrapers traversing the /site viewer so the exact percentage varies quite significantly.

The execution time of the new unranked queries usually sits in the ~5ms range, which is at least an order of magnitude below a full query. A big part of the win is that the unranked queries are single threaded, which frees up many threads from the execution pool to do more meaningful work.

Faster crawls

The crawler’s run time has been creeping up for as long as it’s been creeping and crawling. Lately each partition has been taking almost two weeks to finish, and with 8 main index partitions, that’s nearly four months to do a full crawl! This is a bit long, results get plenty stale in that interval.

The cause of this is a bit unexpected.

The main reason why the crawlers take so long to run is that subdomains are pareto distributed, and crawler politeness demands we don’t hammer websites on the same top domain simultaneously.

A handful of websites (especially substack) are fairly strict on 429:ing you if you hit them too frequently, but even for the rest, it’s better to behave well and keep being allowed to visit, than to burn the IP being greedy and lose the ability to index them altogether.

This makes for one heck of a bottleneck. The main crawl finishes in just a few days really, and then there’s over a week of trickling through substack, medium, wordpress, github.io, neocities.org, etc.

Few puzzles are hard enough that they don’t have a clever solution however, and the solution I arrived at was to add a new crawler partition, to migrate over all these wide domains to this partition, and then do a time-based crawl on this domain, where the wide crawler crawl however many websites it can in a week, then calls it a day to allow the updates to be indexed.

This has worked well. Now the main crawler finishes in like five days instead of two weeks with little bottlenecking, and the wide partition crawler does its thing in parallel to the main crawler.

I don’t know what to put here, so I’ll leave you with homework instead as this is my blog and I can end my posts in whatever non-sequiteur fashion I like.

Can AI accelerationism a be framed as a secular millenarian movement? Is Ken MacLeod correct in that the singularity is just a way of selling the idea of rapture to nerds? Please publish an essay somewhere arguing for or against this framing.

blocks.html_en: There’s been some changes to Marginalia Search:

sentences.en: There’s been some changes to Marginalia Search:
sentences.zh: 旁注搜索发生了一些变化：

blocks.html_en: • Migrated to systemd from docker

sentences.en: Migrated to systemd from docker
sentences.zh: 从 docker 迁移到 systemd

blocks.html_en: • New unranked query endpoint

sentences.en: New unranked query endpoint
sentences.zh: 新的未排名查询端点

blocks.html_en: • Wide domains got their own index for faster crawls

sentences.en: Wide domains got their own index for faster crawls
sentences.zh: 宽域有自己的索引以加快爬行速度

blocks.html_en: In brief, this has removed a lot of operational headaches, reduced expensive queries leaving more computational power for the rest, and cut crawl times in half.

sentences.en: In brief, this has removed a lot of operational headaches, reduced expensive queries leaving more computational power for the rest, and cut crawl times in half.
sentences.zh: 简而言之，这消除了许多操作难题，减少了昂贵的查询，为其余部分留下了更多的计算能力，并将爬网时间缩短了一半。

blocks.html_en: Let’s tackle them in some order.

sentences.en: Let’s tackle them in some order.
sentences.zh: 让我们按某种顺序解决它们。

blocks.html_en: We have docker at home

sentences.en: We have docker at home
sentences.zh: 我们家有docker

blocks.html_en: The system has been migrated off docker compose, and onto bare systemd in production. This has been successful, and solved a number of issues.

sentences.en: The system has been migrated off docker compose, and onto bare systemd in production.
sentences.zh: 该系统已从 docker compose 迁移到生产环境中的裸系统。

sentences.en: This has been successful, and solved a number of issues.
sentences.zh: 这已经取得了成功，并解决了许多问题。

blocks.html_en: There are a few constraints that demand a non-standard deployment for Marginalia.

sentences.en: There are a few constraints that demand a non-standard deployment for Marginalia.
sentences.zh: 有一些限制要求 Marginalia 进行非标准部署。

blocks.html_en: NUMA (non-uniform memory architecture).

sentences.en: NUMA (non-uniform memory architecture).
sentences.zh: NUMA（非统一内存架构）。

blocks.html_en:
The production server has two CPUs, each with their own RAM bank, and while they can reach over into each other’s memory, this comes at a cost.
In many scenarios this isn’t a big deal, but for indexes and databases, generally bottlenecked by RAM bandwidth, this is far from ideal.

sentences.en: The production server has two CPUs, each with their own RAM bank, and while they can reach over into each other’s memory, this comes at a cost.In many scenarios this isn’t a big deal, but for indexes and databases, generally bottlenecked by RAM bandwidth, this is far from ideal.
sentences.zh: 生产服务器有两个 CPU，每个 CPU 都有自己的 RAM 组，虽然它们可以访问彼此的内存，但这是有代价的。在许多情况下，这并不是什么大问题，但对于通常受到 RAM 带宽瓶颈的索引和数据库来说，这远非理想。

blocks.html_en: Process lifecycles.

sentences.en: Process lifecycles.
sentences.zh: 流程生命周期。

blocks.html_en: A search engine is anything but stateless, some processes are fairly heavy and run for weeks, some services are very slow to start, some services need to restart relatively often.

sentences.en: A search engine is anything but stateless, some processes are fairly heavy and run for weeks, some services are very slow to start, some services need to restart relatively often.
sentences.zh: 搜索引擎绝不是无状态的，有些进程相当繁重并且运行数周，有些服务启动非常慢，有些服务需要相对频繁地重新启动。

blocks.html_en:
This all but demands that the search engine is cut up into different pieces.
Not necessarily microservices, but at least services.

sentences.en: This all but demands that the search engine is cut up into different pieces.Not necessarily microservices, but at least services.
sentences.zh: 这几乎要求搜索引擎被分割成不同的部分。不一定是微服务，但至少是服务。

blocks.html_en: IP addresses.

sentences.en: IP addresses.
sentences.zh: IP 地址。

blocks.html_en: The crawler (and crawler-adjacent processes) run off about a dozen public IP addresses, from the same host. This is done via ipvlan using network namespaces.

sentences.en: The crawler (and crawler-adjacent processes) run off about a dozen public IP addresses, from the same host.
sentences.zh: 爬网程序（以及爬网程序相邻进程）从同一主机运行大约十几个公共 IP 地址。

sentences.en: This is done via ipvlan using network namespaces.
sentences.zh: 这是通过使用网络命名空间的 ipvlan 完成的。

blocks.html_en: Linux has many advanced capabilities for giving processes their own virtual network stack, which can be wired together with virtual switches and virtual patch cables. There are to my knowledge no tools for managing this I would consider good, it’s all either too low level, or abstracting away key functionality, or suffering from multiple sources of truths leading to jank when they inevitably differ.

sentences.en: Linux has many advanced capabilities for giving processes their own virtual network stack, which can be wired together with virtual switches and virtual patch cables.
sentences.zh: Linux 具有许多高级功能，可以为进程提供自己的虚拟网络堆栈，该堆栈可以与虚拟交换机和虚拟跳线连接在一起。

sentences.en: There are to my knowledge no tools for managing this I would consider good, it’s all either too low level, or abstracting away key functionality, or suffering from multiple sources of truths leading to jank when they inevitably differ.
sentences.zh: 据我所知，没有任何工具可以用来管理这个问题，我认为这一切都太低级，或者抽象了关键功能，或者遭受多个事实来源的困扰，当它们不可避免地存在差异时导致卡顿。

blocks.html_en: Docker can deal with all of these constraints, but at the expense of considerable jank.

sentences.en: Docker can deal with all of these constraints, but at the expense of considerable jank.
sentences.zh: Docker 可以处理所有这些限制，但代价是相当大的卡顿。

This is in no way a limitation of ipvlans, but purely a docker problem.

sentences.en: Docker’s networking model in particular, while it supports ipvlan, doesn’t map onto it particularly well, if you have a finite subnet you pretty much have to have spare free IPs to be able to reliably restart services.
sentences.zh: 特别是 Docker 的网络模型，虽然它支持 ipvlan，但并不能很好地映射到它，如果你有一个有限的子网，那么你几乎必须有空闲的 IP 才能可靠地重新启动服务。

sentences.en: It further doesn’t let you set firewall rules for the ipvlan interface, which means that anything you put there better not bind on the public IP, or its ass is going to be hanging out in full view.
sentences.zh: 它还不允许你为 ipvlan 接口设置防火墙规则，这意味着你放在那里的任何东西最好不要绑定在公共 IP 上，否则它的屁股将会在众目睽睽之下闲逛。

sentences.en: This is in no way a limitation of ipvlans, but purely a docker problem.
sentences.zh: 这绝不是 ipvlan 的限制，而纯粹是 docker 的问题。

blocks.html_en: This is made worse by the fact that there is inadequate tooling for letting the processes in the container know which network interface is public, so you have to kinda guess based on dowsing rods, RFC 1918, and looking at tea leaves. This is no way to live.

sentences.en: This is made worse by the fact that there is inadequate tooling for letting the processes in the container know which network interface is public, so you have to kinda guess based on dowsing rods, RFC 1918, and looking at tea leaves.
sentences.zh: 更糟糕的是，没有足够的工具让容器中的进程知道哪个网络接口是公共的，因此您必须根据探矿棒、RFC 1918 和查看茶叶进行猜测。

sentences.en: This is no way to live.
sentences.zh: 这不是活路。

blocks.html_en: There is no magic in docker, it’s just cgroups and namespaces all the way down, and you can fully replicate what it’s doing yourself, if you really want to torment yourself using syscalls or shellscripts, or if you want a middle ground using systemd.

sentences.en: There is no magic in docker, it’s just cgroups and namespaces all the way down, and you can fully replicate what it’s doing yourself, if you really want to torment yourself using syscalls or shellscripts, or if you want a middle ground using systemd.
sentences.zh: docker 中没有什么魔力，它只是自始至终的 cgroup 和命名空间，如果你真的想使用系统调用或 shell 脚本来折磨自己，或者如果你想要使用 systemd 来实现中间立场，你可以完全复制它自己正在做的事情。

blocks.html_en: If docker’s problem is that there is a mismatch between the docker model of the system and the system itself, systemd represents a more raw approach, where a lot more of the configuration is in your hands, while still offering many capabilities you’d want for a more serious deployment, such as health checks, automatic restarts that can be configured.

sentences.en: If docker’s problem is that there is a mismatch between the docker model of the system and the system itself, systemd represents a more raw approach, where a lot more of the configuration is in your hands, while still offering many capabilities you’d want for a more serious deployment, such as health checks, automatic restarts that can be configured.

sentences.zh: 部署速度更快，服务发现中的卡顿更少，因为“容器”保留相同的内部和外部 IP，整个操作感觉不那么浮动。

sentences.en: Good upgrade.
sentences.zh: 很好的升级。

blocks.html_en: There are many opinions about systemd, and I think for most desktop-type systems, it’s incredibly overengineered and kind of a pain to work with. Though for something like this, its design is well motivated, and it really shines.

sentences.en: The query execution pipeline used in the search engine is fairly computationally expensive, and does things that aren’t strictly necessary in many queries.
sentences.zh: 搜索引擎中使用的查询执行管道的计算成本相当高，并且执行许多查询中并非严格必要的操作。

blocks.html_en: If you’re for example just looking for backlinks, a pure intersection of the terms ‘site:foo.com links:bar.com’ is enough, there’s nothing to rank anything there, but things working the way they did, threads were allocated to ranking none the less, term positions were attempted to be retrieved, all a bunch of done that needed not to be done.

sentences.en: If you’re for example just looking for backlinks, a pure intersection of the terms ‘site:foo.com links:bar.com’ is enough, there’s nothing to rank anything there, but things working the way they did, threads were allocated to ranking none the less, term positions were attempted to be retrieved, all a bunch of done that needed not to be done.
sentences.zh: 例如，如果您只是寻找反向链接，那么术语“site:foo.com links:bar.com”的纯粹交集就足够了，那里没有任何东西可以对任何内容进行排名，但是事情按照它们的方式工作，线程被分配到排名，尝试检索术语位置，所有这些都不需要完成。

blocks.html_en: So I added a path for unranked queries that just do dumb term intersections and a bare minimum of anything else.

sentences.en: So I added a path for unranked queries that just do dumb term intersections and a bare minimum of anything else.
sentences.zh: 因此，我为未排序的查询添加了一条路径，该查询仅执行愚蠢的术语交叉和最少的其他操作。

blocks.html_en: The bonus of this is that with some fiddling and the construction of a cursor that can map to multiple index partitions, unlike the ranked path, unranked queries can be exhaustively retrieved with minimal additional work.

sentences.en: The bonus of this is that with some fiddling and the construction of a cursor that can map to multiple index partitions, unlike the ranked path, unranked queries can be exhaustively retrieved with minimal additional work.
sentences.zh: 这样做的好处是，与排名路径不同，通过一些摆弄和构建可以映射到多个索引分区的游标，可以用最少的额外工作彻底检索未排名的查询。

blocks.html_en: Keeping track of the position across multiple partitions to allow exhaustive retrieval took some thinking, but I designed a cursor that looks like this, that can be passed to the clients:

sentences.en: Keeping track of the position across multiple partitions to allow exhaustive retrieval took some thinking, but I designed a cursor that looks like this, that can be passed to the clients:
sentences.zh: 跟踪多个分区的位置以允许详尽的检索需要一些思考，但我设计了一个如下所示的游标，可以将其传递给客户端：

blocks.html_en: Each period-delimited part starts with a single character mapping to an index partition, and then a string of alphanumerical values that’s a base 36-encoded document id, the document id to resume from on that partition. It’s short enough to pass on a query string or along an API query, which is really all that matters.

sentences.en: Each period-delimited part starts with a single character mapping to an index partition, and then a string of alphanumerical values that’s a base 36-encoded document id, the document id to resume from on that partition.
sentences.zh: 每个以句点分隔的部分都以映射到索引分区的单个字符开始，然后是一串字母数字值，它是基本 36 编码的文档 ID，即从该分区上恢复的文档 ID。

sentences.en: It’s short enough to pass on a query string or along an API query, which is really all that matters.
sentences.zh: 它足够短，可以传递查询字符串或 API 查询，这才是最重要的。

blocks.html_en: Implementing this allows up to half of the query load to go to the new endpoint, though many of these queries are driven by bots and scrapers traversing the /site viewer so the exact percentage varies quite significantly.

sentences.en: Implementing this allows up to half of the query load to go to the new endpoint, though many of these queries are driven by bots and scrapers traversing the /site viewer so the exact percentage varies quite significantly.
sentences.zh: 实现此功能允许最多一半的查询负载转到新端点，尽管其中许多查询是由遍历 /site 查看器的机器人和抓取工具驱动的，因此确切的百分比变化很大。

blocks.html_en: The execution time of the new unranked queries usually sits in the ~5ms range, which is at least an order of magnitude below a full query. A big part of the win is that the unranked queries are single threaded, which frees up many threads from the execution pool to do more meaningful work.

sentences.en: The execution time of the new unranked queries usually sits in the ~5ms range, which is at least an order of magnitude below a full query.
sentences.zh: 新的未排序查询的执行时间通常在约 5 毫秒范围内，这至少比完整查询低一个数量级。

sentences.en: A big part of the win is that the unranked queries are single threaded, which frees up many threads from the execution pool to do more meaningful work.
sentences.zh: 胜利的一个重要部分是未排序的查询是单线程的，这可以从执行池中释放许多线程来执行更有意义的工作。

blocks.html_en: Faster crawls

sentences.en: Faster crawls
sentences.zh: 更快的爬行

blocks.html_en: The crawler’s run time has been creeping up for as long as it’s been creeping and crawling. Lately each partition has been taking almost two weeks to finish, and with 8 main index partitions, that’s nearly four months to do a full crawl! This is a bit long, results get plenty stale in that interval.

sentences.en: The crawler’s run time has been creeping up for as long as it’s been creeping and crawling.
sentences.zh: 只要爬行爬行，爬虫的运行时间就一直在爬行。

sentences.en: Lately each partition has been taking almost two weeks to finish, and with 8 main index partitions, that’s nearly four months to do a full crawl!
sentences.zh: 最近，每个分区都花费了近两周的时间才能完成，而对于 8 个主索引分区来说，完成一次完整的爬行需要近四个月的时间！

sentences.en: This is a bit long, results get plenty stale in that interval.
sentences.zh: 这有点长，在这段时间内结果变得很陈旧。

blocks.html_en: The cause of this is a bit unexpected.

sentences.en: The cause of this is a bit unexpected.
sentences.zh: 这件事的起因有点出人意料。

blocks.html_en: The main reason why the crawlers take so long to run is that subdomains are pareto distributed, and crawler politeness demands we don’t hammer websites on the same top domain simultaneously.

sentences.en: The main reason why the crawlers take so long to run is that subdomains are pareto distributed, and crawler politeness demands we don’t hammer websites on the same top domain simultaneously.
sentences.zh: 爬虫运行时间这么长的主要原因是子域是帕累托分布的，爬虫礼貌要求我们不要同时攻击同一个顶级域上的网站。

blocks.html_en: <th>cnt</th>
<th>domain_top</th>

sentences.en: domain_top
sentences.zh: 域名顶部

blocks.html_en: tumblr.com

sentences.en: tumblr.com

blocks.html_en: blogspot.com

sentences.en: blogspot.com

blocks.html_en: wordpress.com

sentences.en: wordpress.com

blocks.html_en: uptodown.com

sentences.en: uptodown.com

blocks.html_en: bandcamp.com

sentences.en: bandcamp.com

blocks.html_en: livejournal.com

sentences.en: livejournal.com

blocks.html_en: appstor.io

sentences.en: appstor.io

blocks.html_en: github.io

sentences.en: github.io

blocks.html_en: substack.com

sentences.en: substack.com

blocks.html_en: dreamwidth.org

sentences.en: dreamwidth.org

blocks.html_en: medium.com

sentences.en: medium.com

blocks.html_en: wixsite.com

sentences.en: wixsite.com

blocks.html_en: <figcaption>Table: Worst offenders for known subdomains per top domain</figcaption>

sentences.en: Table: Worst offenders for known subdomains per top domain
sentences.zh: 表：每个顶级域的已知子域的最严重违规者

blocks.html_en: A handful of websites (especially substack) are fairly strict on 429:ing you if you hit them too frequently, but even for the rest, it’s better to behave well and keep being allowed to visit, than to burn the IP being greedy and lose the ability to index them altogether.

sentences.en: A handful of websites (especially substack) are fairly strict on 429:ing you if you hit them too frequently, but even for the rest, it’s better to behave well and keep being allowed to visit, than to burn the IP being greedy and lose the ability to index them altogether.
sentences.zh: 少数网站（尤其是 substack）对 429:ing 相当严格，如果你太频繁地访问它们，但即使对于其他网站，最好表现良好并继续被允许访问，而不是贪婪地烧掉 IP 并失去对它们进行索引的能力。

blocks.html_en: This makes for one heck of a bottleneck. The main crawl finishes in just a few days really, and then there’s over a week of trickling through substack, medium, wordpress, github.io, neocities.org, etc.

sentences.en: This makes for one heck of a bottleneck.
sentences.zh: 这造成了一个瓶颈。

sentences.en: The main crawl finishes in just a few days really, and then there’s over a week of trickling through substack, medium, wordpress, github.io, neocities.org, etc.
sentences.zh: 主要的爬行实际上在短短几天内就完成了，然后有一个多星期的时间通过 substack、medium、wordpress、github.io、neocities.org 等进行爬行。

blocks.html_en: Few puzzles are hard enough that they don’t have a clever solution however, and the solution I arrived at was to add a new crawler partition, to migrate over all these wide domains to this partition, and then do a time-based crawl on this domain, where the wide crawler crawl however many websites it can in a week, then calls it a day to allow the updates to be indexed.

sentences.en: Few puzzles are hard enough that they don’t have a clever solution however, and the solution I arrived at was to add a new crawler partition, to migrate over all these wide domains to this partition, and then do a time-based crawl on this domain, where the wide crawler crawl however many websites it can in a week, then calls it a day to allow the updates to be indexed.
sentences.zh: 然而，很少有难题足够困难，他们没有一个聪明的解决方案，我得到的解决方案是添加一个新的爬虫分区，将所有这些宽域迁移到这个分区，然后在这个域上进行基于时间的爬行，其中宽爬虫在一周内爬行尽可能多的网站，然后收工以允许对更新建立索引。

blocks.html_en: This has worked well. Now the main crawler finishes in like five days instead of two weeks with little bottlenecking, and the wide partition crawler does its thing in parallel to the main crawler.

sentences.en: This has worked well.
sentences.zh: 这效果很好。

sentences.en: Now the main crawler finishes in like five days instead of two weeks with little bottlenecking, and the wide partition crawler does its thing in parallel to the main crawler.
sentences.zh: 现在，主爬网程序在大约五天内完成，而不是两周，几乎没有瓶颈，并且宽分区爬网程序与主爬网程序并行执行其操作。

blocks.html_en: I don’t know what to put here, so I’ll leave you with homework instead as this is my blog and I can end my posts in whatever non-sequiteur fashion I like.

sentences.en: I don’t know what to put here, so I’ll leave you with homework instead as this is my blog and I can end my posts in whatever non-sequiteur fashion I like.
sentences.zh: 我不知道该放在这里什么，所以我会给你留一些家庭作业，因为这是我的博客，我可以用我喜欢的任何不合逻辑的方式结束我的帖子。

blocks.html_en: Can AI accelerationism a be framed as a secular millenarian movement? Is Ken MacLeod correct in that the singularity is just a way of selling the idea of rapture to nerds? Please publish an essay somewhere arguing for or against this framing.

sentences.en: Can AI accelerationism a be framed as a secular millenarian movement?
sentences.zh: 人工智能加速主义可以被定义为世俗的千禧年运动吗？

sentences.en: Is Ken MacLeod correct in that the singularity is just a way of selling the idea of rapture to nerds?
sentences.zh: 肯·麦克劳德（Ken MacLeod）认为奇点只是向书呆子兜售狂喜观念的一种方式，这是否正确？

sentences.en: Please publish an essay somewhere arguing for or against this framing.
sentences.zh: 请在某个地方发表一篇文章来支持或反对这个框架。

content_html_zh:
旁注搜索发生了一些变化：

  • 从 docker 迁移到 systemd
  • 新的未排名查询端点
  • 宽域有自己的索引以加快爬行速度

简而言之，这消除了许多操作难题，减少了昂贵的查询，为其余部分留下了更多的计算能力，并将爬网时间缩短了一半。

让我们按某种顺序解决它们。

我们家有docker

该系统已从 docker compose 迁移到生产环境中的裸系统。 这已经取得了成功，并解决了许多问题。

有一些限制要求 Marginalia 进行非标准部署。

NUMA（非统一内存架构）。

生产服务器有两个 CPU，每个 CPU 都有自己的 RAM 组，虽然它们可以访问彼此的内存，但这是有代价的。在许多情况下，这并不是什么大问题，但对于通常受到 RAM 带宽瓶颈的索引和数据库来说，这远非理想。

流程生命周期。

搜索引擎绝不是无状态的，有些进程相当繁重并且运行数周，有些服务启动非常慢，有些服务需要相对频繁地重新启动。

这几乎要求搜索引擎被分割成不同的部分。不一定是微服务，但至少是服务。

IP 地址。

爬网程序（以及爬网程序相邻进程）从同一主机运行大约十几个公共 IP 地址。 这是通过使用网络命名空间的 ipvlan 完成的。

Linux 具有许多高级功能，可以为进程提供自己的虚拟网络堆栈，该堆栈可以与虚拟交换机和虚拟跳线连接在一起。 据我所知，没有任何工具可以用来管理这个问题，我认为这一切都太低级，或者抽象了关键功能，或者遭受多个事实来源的困扰，当它们不可避免地存在差异时导致卡顿。

Docker 可以处理所有这些限制，但代价是相当大的卡顿。

特别是 Docker 的网络模型，虽然它支持 ipvlan，但并不能很好地映射到它，如果你有一个有限的子网，那么你几乎必须有空闲的 IP 才能可靠地重新启动服务。 它还不允许你为 ipvlan 接口设置防火墙规则，这意味着你放在那里的任何东西最好不要绑定在公共 IP 上，否则它的屁股将会在众目睽睽之下闲逛。 这绝不是 ipvlan 的限制，而纯粹是 docker 的问题。

更糟糕的是，没有足够的工具让容器中的进程知道哪个网络接口是公共的，因此您必须根据探矿棒、RFC 1918 和查看茶叶进行猜测。 这不是活路。

docker 中没有什么魔力，它只是自始至终的 cgroup 和命名空间，如果你真的想使用系统调用或 shell 脚本来折磨自己，或者如果你想要使用 systemd 来实现中间立场，你可以完全复制它自己正在做的事情。

如果 docker 的问题是系统的 docker 模型与系统本身不匹配，那么 systemd 代表了一种更原始​​的方法，其中更多的配置掌握在您手中，同时仍然提供您需要进行更严格的部署的许多功能，例如运行状况检查、可配置的自动重启。

设置一百万个 .service:s 是相当乏味的，但直接插入会有所帮助。 这里没什么可说的，除了经过一些摆弄之外，它运行得很好，并且感觉比 docker 以前的稳定得多。 将 JIB 从构建中删除意味着构建现在大部分时间需要 2-3 秒。

部署速度更快，服务发现中的卡顿更少，因为“容器”保留相同的内部和外部 IP，整个操作感觉不那么浮动。 很好的升级。

关于 systemd 有很多观点，我认为对于大多数桌面类型的系统来说，它的设计过度得令人难以置信，并且使用起来有点痛苦。 虽然对于这样的东西来说，它的设计动机很好，而且确实很耀眼。

未排序的查询

索引现在允许执行未排序的查询！

搜索引擎中使用的查询执行管道的计算成本相当高，并且执行许多查询中并非严格必要的操作。

例如，如果您只是寻找反向链接，那么术语“site:foo.com links:bar.com”的纯粹交集就足够了，那里没有任何东西可以对任何内容进行排名，但是事情按照它们的方式工作，线程被分配到排名，尝试检索术语位置，所有这些都不需要完成。

因此，我为未排序的查询添加了一条路径，该查询仅执行愚蠢的术语交叉和最少的其他操作。

这样做的好处是，与排名路径不同，通过一些摆弄和构建可以映射到多个索引分区的游标，可以用最少的额外工作彻底检索未排名的查询。

跟踪多个分区的位置以允许详尽的检索需要一些思考，但我设计了一个如下所示的游标，可以将其传递给客户端：

28mbshfptkj.6ijoop7xty.43nivb3bn1.817ucldxy2x.90.12ria2fmp7a.32cydk0dei2t.73wrhi4igjr.53lb1o4iof7

每个以句点分隔的部分都以映射到索引分区的单个字符开始，然后是一串字母数字值，它是基本 36 编码的文档 ID，即从该分区上恢复的文档 ID。 它足够短，可以传递查询字符串或 API 查询，这才是最重要的。

实现此功能允许最多一半的查询负载转到新端点，尽管其中许多查询是由遍历 /site 查看器的机器人和抓取工具驱动的，因此确切的百分比变化很大。

新的未排序查询的执行时间通常在约 5 毫秒范围内，这至少比完整查询低一个数量级。 胜利的一个重要部分是未排序的查询是单线程的，这可以从执行池中释放许多线程来执行更有意义的工作。

更快的爬行

只要爬行爬行，爬虫的运行时间就一直在爬行。 最近，每个分区都花费了近两周的时间才能完成，而对于 8 个主索引分区来说，完成一次完整的爬行需要近四个月的时间！ 这有点长，在这段时间内结果变得很陈旧。

这件事的起因有点出人意料。

爬虫运行时间这么长的主要原因是子域是帕累托分布的，爬虫礼貌要求我们不要同时攻击同一个顶级域上的网站。

少数网站（尤其是 substack）对 429:ing 相当严格，如果你太频繁地访问它们，但即使对于其他网站，最好表现良好并继续被允许访问，而不是贪婪地烧掉 IP 并失去对它们进行索引的能力。

这造成了一个瓶颈。 主要的爬行实际上在短短几天内就完成了，然后有一个多星期的时间通过 substack、medium、wordpress、github.io、neocities.org 等进行爬行。

然而，很少有难题足够困难，他们没有一个聪明的解决方案，我得到的解决方案是添加一个新的爬虫分区，将所有这些宽域迁移到这个分区，然后在这个域上进行基于时间的爬行，其中宽爬虫在一周内爬行尽可能多的网站，然后收工以允许对更新建立索引。

这效果很好。 现在，主爬网程序在大约五天内完成，而不是两周，几乎没有瓶颈，并且宽分区爬网程序与主爬网程序并行执行其操作。

我不知道该放在这里什么，所以我会给你留一些家庭作业，因为这是我的博客，我可以用我喜欢的任何不合逻辑的方式结束我的帖子。

人工智能加速主义可以被定义为世俗的千禧年运动吗？ 肯·麦克劳德（Ken MacLeod）认为奇点只是向书呆子兜售狂喜观念的一种方式，这是否正确？ 请在某个地方发表一篇文章来支持或反对这个框架。
